prompt:
## Intent

Generate a test for the login page

## Desired Outcome

Generate a test for the login page in the intent-poc-app.

## Repo Context

- The prompt suggests a user-facing change in intent-poc-app, but it does not unambiguously identify which code surface owns it.
- Intent Driven Development POC: Current workspace source for Intent Studio, surface library evidence flows, and built-in surface library screenshots.
- Open routing question: Prompt normalization identified Shared Source as the most likely code surface within intent-poc-app.

## Source Scope

- intent-poc-app
- Selected source: intent-poc-app.
- Likely owning surface: Shared Source (medium confidence).
- Fallback surfaces to inspect next: Intent Studio and Surface Library.

## Adaptive Boundaries

- No concrete owning file was identified from the prompt yet.
- Keep this draft inside intent-poc-app until the owning surface is confirmed.
- Resolve whether the change belongs to Intent Studio and Surface Library before touching multiple UI surfaces.

## Minimum Success

- A test for the login page is successfully generated.
- Generate a test for the login page in the intent-poc-app.
- The draft keeps the change centered on Shared Source instead of the whole repo.
- The final plan names one existing UI verification path instead of inventing a new workflow.

## Baseline

- Capture the current baseline in Shared Source before changing behavior.

## Verification Obligations

- Name the existing Playwright or screenshot check that will prove the behavior change.

## Raw Intent

Generate a test for the login page

normalizedIntent.rawPrompt:
Generate a test for the login page

Desired outcome: Generate a test for the login page in the intent-poc-app.

codeSurface.label: Surface Library
codeSurface.rationale: Prompt normalization identified Surface Library as the most likely code surface within intent-poc-app.

alternatives.label: Intent Studio
alternatives.reason: Prompt normalization kept this as a fallback interpretation.

alternatives.label: Shared Source
alternatives.reason: Prompt normalization kept this as a fallback interpretation.

businessIntent.statement: Generate a Playwright test for the login page in intent-poc-app.
businessIntent.desiredOutcome: A functional Playwright test script is added to intent-poc-app that validates login page elements and captures theme-aware screenshots for visual verification.

acceptanceCriteria.description: Playwright test file is created within the intent-poc-app source.
Test validates the existence of login form primitives including username/email input, password input, and login button.
Test captures visual evidence for both light and dark modes using the 'theme-mode' query parameter.
Test execution produces a passing result and generates reviewable screenshot artifacts.

scenarios.title: Validate Login Form Elements
scenarios.goal: Ensure the login page contains necessary UI components for user authentication.
given: A Playwright test environment is configured
when: The test navigates to the login page route
The test queries for username and password inputs
The test queries for the submit button
then: All required form elements are found in the DOM
The elements are visible and interactable

scenarios.title: Capture Theme-Aware Visual Evidence
scenarios.goal: Verify the login page appearance across different themes to ensure visual consistency.
given: The intent-poc-app supports the 'theme-mode' query parameter
The login page route is defined
when: The test captures a screenshot at the login route with 'dark=false'
The test captures a screenshot at the login route with 'dark=true'
then: Light mode screenshots are saved as reference evidence
Dark mode screenshots are saved as reference evidence
Visual contrast is maintained in both theme states

workItems.title: Login Page Validation and Visual Evidence
workItems.description: Author a Playwright test to validate the login page elements and capture theme-aware screenshots for light and dark modes.
workItems.userVisibleOutcome: A functional Playwright test script validates the login form and provides visual evidence for both light and dark themes.
workItems.verification: Run the Playwright spec 'intent-poc-app/login-page-validation.spec.ts' and verify that all assertions pass and screenshots are generated for both theme states.

specs.testName: Validate Login Form and Theme Consistency

checkpoints.label: Navigate to Login Page (Light Mode)
checkpoints.assertion: The login page loads successfully with the light theme applied.

requiredUiStates.label: Theme mode
requiredUiStates.description: The demo app supports light and dark theme states that materially change contrast, tokens, and screenshot evidence.

notes: The demo app now honors the dark query parameter across Studio and library routes for deterministic verification.
The library surface toggle remains available for manual verification flows.
Intent Studio keeps an explicit toggle for interactive review during manual sessions.
Stateful UI bugs should declare and verify the requested UI state before screenshot evidence is trusted.
Prefer reusable state activation contracts over prompt-specific routing or one-off checkpoint logic.
Theme-sensitive UI bugs require paired light-mode and dark-mode screenshot evidence; do not accept a dark-mode-only capture set.
Verify the active theme state before trusting screenshot comparisons.
Prefer the shared dark query parameter during generated verification so screenshots start in the requested theme.
Dark-theme verification should keep a light-mode reference screenshot alongside the dark-mode target evidence.
requiredUiStates.reason: Prompt requests the Theme mode UI state with value "light".

destinations.label: Controller artifacts
destinations.reason: Local evidence bundles are always written by the controller.
details: Stores plan, manifests, logs, captures, and summaries on disk.

destinations.label: Source workspace publication
destinations.reason: Workspace publishing is disabled until artifacts.storageMode is set to 'both'.
details: Useful when evidence should live beside the source repository.

tools.label: BDD planning
tools.reason: The system converts the raw prompt into reviewable acceptance criteria and scenarios during the full reviewed plan pass.
details: Deterministic by default, with optional Gemini refinement when configured.

tools.label: Playwright TDD generation
tools.reason: Applicable sources now produce Playwright-first executable test plans and checkpoint screenshots.
details: Generated specs are intended for checked-in repo storage with overwrite semantics.

tools.label: Visual verification
tools.reason: Applicable sources currently expose visual checkpoints and capture definitions.
details: Playwright screenshots remain one execution tool rather than the top-level product model.

tools.label: Environment deployment
tools.reason: A generic local or Kubernetes deployment stage is planned but not yet wired into the runner.
details: This will become the shared path for local, minikube, and k3s-backed execution.

tools.label: Implementation loop
tools.reason: The runner can execute bounded implementation attempts against the prepared source workspace.
details: QA failures can feed directly back into the next bounded implementation attempt.

tools.label: QA verification
tools.reason: The runner can now execute a bounded QA bundle before evidence capture completes the lane.
details: The default QA bundle runs typecheck, the deterministic code test suite, and generated Playwright specs when a source enables them.

tools.label: Evidence reporting
tools.reason: Every run produces summaries and machine-readable manifests.
details: Reports become distribution inputs for downstream review and publishing surfaces.
